finding: Individual differences in broad personality dimensions show systematic associations with regional variation in cortical thickness, surface area, and folding geometry, particularly in prefrontal regions, suggesting anatomical substrate for trait-linked behavioral dispositions.

Early low-status assignment through grade retention causally reduces odds of secondary educational milestone completion through mechanisms operating in middle school achievement and expectations, establishing a scarring process where initial deficits accumulate over time.

Temporal framing of future opportunity causally drives preference for immediate social goal pursuit over exploratory advancement when horizons contract, with expanded time horizons eliminating age-typical goal prioritization.

Size of online social network membership reliably predicts regional brain structure in areas supporting social perception and associative memory encoding, distinct from structures supporting real-world intimate network size.
Humans maintain preference for coordinated joint action despite instrumentally higher costs of synchronization, indicating intrinsic reward value beyond utility maximization in cooperation decisions.

Code comment presence affects perceived readability independent of comprehension measured by recall accuracy; method chaining shows no consistent readability or comprehension effect; experience level differentiates performance but not perceived readability.
